description: Benchmark targets: pinned configs + shard maps per issues #110/#133/#193/#202, collapsed to a SINGLE live per-merge configuration per issue #250 (espg-approved restructure on PR #256): o9, hive store layout, sharded output, tdigest, inline read, no AOI mask, granule_workers=4, 4 GB. The former inline/sidecar x mask/nomask 2x2 (issue #202 reset) is retired to provisional_targets (runnable by explicit --target, frozen rows retained in the series; its figures move to the Archived section). The live target doubles as the write-path regression arm: the object-count tripwire hard-fails the merge run on a sharded-write bypass (issue #215). Every-merge run dispatches ONE pinned densest shard (run_benchmark.py) with profile=True so the worker phase split (read/index/aggregate/write) lands in the series.

healpix_o11.note: Re-pinned over the full-mission window 2018-10-13..2026-03-15 (issue #202): the densest o11 shard is now 5347398660508352523 at 50 granules, with a close cluster of three shards at 49 (incl. the old pin 5347394812217655307). The benchmark always dispatches this static shard_key, so the series is stable regardless of CMR drift. The drift test is tie-tolerant (compares granule count within +/-1, not exact shard_key) so an equally-dense reselection over the window doesn't read as drift.

healpix_o10.note: Re-pinned over 2018-10-13..2026-03-15 (issue #202). Retired from the live matrix (o9-only reset); retained as a frozen provisional target so its shard-map pin stays drift-guarded.

healpix_o9.note: Re-pinned over 2018-10-13..2026-03-15 (issue #202): densest o9 shard unchanged (5347395636851376137), granule count 59 -> 66. BOTH live-matrix arms dispatch this plain map: the nomask arm as-is; the mask arm builds the strict-AOI per-cell mask on the fly at dispatch (run_benchmark._shardmap_with_mask, mortie) -- the ~190 KB mask is derived (grid + AOI, doesn't move granules), so it is not committed (issue #202).

healpix_o9_88s.note: 88S stress pin (issue #148): the worst shard by granule count over the full turning-latitude ring (576 o9 shards, 2.04M pairs, 35,639 catalog granules). The committed map is PRUNED to this one shard (metadata.pruned); the full map is ~0.7 GB. Expected to OOM/timeout at 2 GB -- that is the point (see README).

healpix_o10_88s.note: 88S stress pin (issue #148), NESTED: the densest o10 shard inside the pinned o9 stress shard (nested_in), not the global o10 densest (4,642 at another ring longitude), so one o9 extraction pass covers both orders per the issue #148 decisions. The drift test derives the nested densest when nested_in is set. Committed map PRUNED to this shard (full map ~1.7 GB).

tdigest_healpix_o9_hive._comment: The whole per-merge leg (issue #250 collapse): o9 t-digest, store_layout: hive, sharded, pinned densest shard, inline read path, no AOI mask. Still the write-path regression arm (object-count tripwire hard-fails on a sharded-write bypass -- issue #215).

provisional_targets._comment: PROVISIONAL, PR-TREE-ONLY (issue #130). The two scalar targets share ONE config + ONE shard (healpix_o11) and differ only by handoff (pandas / arrow) to benchmark the per-cell carrier cost: pandas DataFrame vs an arro3-core Arrow table. Both produce byte-identical scalar output and neither imports pyarrow. (The experimental arrow-kernel reducer was dropped with pyarrow per @espg's path-C decision https://github.com/englacial/zagg/pull/131#issuecomment-4838785058, since arro3 has no hash-aggregate.) The two *_88s targets are the issue #148 stress probes: on-demand runs against the pinned worst 88S ring shard, expected to OOM/timeout until the streaming/cached-read work lands -- they must NOT join the every-merge matrix (a red benchmark job every merge). The two tdigest_healpix_o10_{inline,sidecar} entries are the FROZEN o10 read-backend A/B (issue #202 reset): o10 dropped out of the live every-merge matrix (now o9-only) but is retained here -- runnable on demand, its shard-map pin still drift-guarded -- rather than deleted (archived, not rerun). All entries here are deliberately NOT in 'targets': run_benchmark.py runs them by explicit --target name (via /benchmark); 'run all' (no --target) skips them. The *_88s_cached companions (issue #170) are the arm the base _88s probes were waiting for: same pinned 88S shard, read through the sidecar/compiled path (self-populating manifests) -- the base _88s targets stay pinned to the hierarchical baseline (issue #148 arm 1, the honest uncached reference), so the pair measures exactly the cached-vs-uncached delta at the pole. The tdigest_healpix_o9_{inline,sidecar}_{mask,nomask} 2x2 (issue #202 reset) was retired from the live matrix by the issue #250 collapse (per-merge leg = the single hive config); retained here so /benchmark --target can still run an arm on demand.

_frozen_note: Pre-#193 targets (sharded/inner codec A/B + *_cached read axis) are retired from the live matrix as of issue #193; their series rows + PNGs are retained/frozen on the benchmarks branch. As of the issue #202 reset the live matrix is tdigest, sharded, K=4, o9 ONLY -- a 2x2 over inline-vs-sidecar x AOI-mask-vs-nomask. The o10 read-backend rows are frozen (moved to provisional_targets, retained not rerun). The pre-reset live datapoints on the benchmarks branch are stale under the new axes; the corrected series starts fresh -- plot_series only renders rows whose target matches the reset scheme, so the 2x2 begins from zero at the first post-reset merge (a physical prune of the benchmarks-branch series.parquet, if wanted, is an @espg action since agent pushes are scoped to claude/*).
